title: ISE IoT/OT Device Onboarding — Profile Match and SGT Assignment Validation

compliance.assurance_rationale: AC-3 access enforcement — IoT SGT-based enforcement.
A.8.22 segregation of networks — IoT segregation by SGT.
Art.21(2)(d) access control — IoT access control.
compliance.controlObjective: Access-control evidence for IoT and unmanaged endpoint authorization decisions.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Connect a new IoT device that profiles into an unmapped category. UC-17.1.68 captures the gap.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Connect a known IoT type with a defined SGT. No fire.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Profiler* + *Authorization* — combination tracking: a new endpoint MAC seen for the first time, profiled into a category, then assigned an SGT. Cross-reference against `iot_device_inventory.csv` lookup for known IoT/OT classes.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (80001, 5400)
| transaction endpoint_mac maxspan=10m
| stats count by endpoint_type, assigned_sgt
| where isnull(assigned_sgt) OR assigned_sgt=="unknown"
description: Tracks IoT/OT endpoints whose profile is detected but no SGT is assigned (policy gap) or whose profile-to-SGT mapping appears wrong.
value: IoT/OT environments expand rapidly. Endpoints that profile but don't receive an SGT fall through to default policy — usually wider access than intended. Catching the gap allows policy tuning.
implementation: Schedule the search every 1 hour over the last 24 hours; alert when an unauthorised IoT endpoint is observed; throttle by MAC for 24 hours.
visualization: (1) Profile-to-SGT matrix; cells without SGT highlighted. (2) Trend of unassigned-SGT endpoints.
knownFalsePositives: (a) Newly added device class — policy lag before SGT defined.
mitreAttack: N/A (behavioural analytics health — measures ISE AI Endpoint Behavioural model freshness, no attack mapping)
grandmaExplanation: When an IoT device — a sensor, a camera — joins the network, ISE should put it into the right group. We watch for devices that don't get a group assigned, because they end up with wider access than intended.
